danger_signals.visual_form: Cars, skateboarders, rolling tires animated across the street
danger_signals.meaning: Collision hazard; contact costs player one life

danger_signals.visual_form: Walls, ditches, holes rendered in street terrain
danger_signals.meaning: Crash obstacles that destroy player momentum and cost lives

danger_signals.visual_form: Houses without bright color distinction from subscriber homes
danger_signals.meaning: Damage to these properties adds points but breaks subscription contracts if hit

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated characters (angry lady, dog, burglar, motorcyclist) with hostile movement patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Mobile obstacles that intercept player or create unpredictable hazards

safety_signals.visual_form: Houses decorated in bright colors to distinguish from non-subscribers
safety_signals.meaning: Delivery targets; successful throws here maintain/increase subscription count

safety_signals.visual_form: Pickup items placed on ground throughout route
safety_signals.meaning: Resource replenishment; player must collect to continue throwing

safety_signals.visual_form: Dedicated obstacle course section after main route completion
safety_signals.meaning: Safe practice area with unlimited papers; crashing here ends course but causes no life loss

safety_signals.visual_form: Advancement from Monday through Sunday levels with visual day markers
safety_signals.meaning: Forward movement indicates survival; reaching Sunday endpoint triggers victory bonus

faction_indicators.color_identity: Bright colors (exact palette unspecified in sources)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Houses positioned along delivery route; visual distinction from non-subscriber dwellings

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Undistinguished house appearance (absence of bright color marker)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Default/plain houses; damage bonus points indicate adversarial relationship

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Animated character sprites: angry lady, dog, burglar, motorcyclist, skateboarder
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Burglar has window-breaking animation; angry lady has running/angry pose; dog has chase behavior; motorcyclist drives between blocks
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mobile hazards appearing contextually on street (house-based lady, yard-based dog, street-crossing motorcyclist)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: House displays bright color; mailbox accessible as throw target
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Successful delivery adds subscribers (up to max 10); failed delivery or damage removes subscription

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: House reverts to undistinguished appearance or disappears from delivery route
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: House no longer counts as delivery target; point value lost

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Collision animation; possible explosion graphic (unused but indicates intent)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player respawns or game-over screen displays; route resets or ends

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level advances from Monday to Sunday; Sunday papers render visually heavier/slower trajectory
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Throwable papers travel more slowly on Sunday; obstacle patterns intensify across week

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Obstacle course terrain (targets, ramps, walls, ditches) replaces normal route
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unlimited paper supply; no subscription penalties; crashing ends course without life loss

environmental_storytelling.example: Bright color decoration on 10 of 20 houses establishes economic class/affluence status and reading audience identification; non-colored houses suggest poverty or disinterest in newspaper service
Angry lady emerges from house (domestic confrontation); dog chases from yard (territory defense); burglar at window (crime/theft threat); motorcyclist crosses street (reckless youth culture)
Cars, skateboarders, rolling tires convey suburban street dangers familiar to delivery-route children; contextualizes paperboy as vulnerable adolescent in unpredictable environment
Monday–Sunday level structure with visual day markers and mechanical change (heavier papers on Sunday) establishes work-week fatigue and mounting challenge narrative without dialogue

Point rewards for breaking non-subscriber windows frames vandalism as economically incentivized; communicates world where property damage to 'unworthy' targets is celebrated, establishing morally ambiguous protagonist

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Runs out of house in aggressive pose; collision with paperboy costs life; unused graphics show 'downed state' frame suggesting combat intent
Chases paperboy; animated rough sprite shows chase movement; collision hazard
Drives between blocks in predictable path; collision hazard; unused early sprite shows motorcycle motion
Attempts window break-in (unused but designed); represents theft threat; contextualizes 'mysterious thief' paperboy supposedly stops
Throw animation completes; subscription count increases; unused end-course animation shows cap removal (victory pose)
Crashes into obstacle; collision animation triggers; life lost or course ends

notes: Paperboy communicates world-state primarily through visual subscriber/non-subscriber house distinction and NPC hazard placement. The game lacks explicit faction narrative but establishes implicit social structure: subscribers (affluent, protected) vs. non-subscribers (targets for vandalism). Danger manifests through both mechanical obstacles (cars, terrain) and character-based threats (angry lady, dog, burglar). Environmental storytelling relies on NPC context positioning and daily progression cycle. Regional differences (US vs. Japan) show cultural localization of protagonist appearance but no substantive changes to danger/safety/faction signaling. Unused graphics (burglar, expanded NPC roster, explosion effects) suggest original design included more explicit combat/crime narrative, removed in final NES port.
sources_used: tcrf - Paperboy (NES) - The Cutting Room Floor
